title: The Product Manager Handbook
subtitle: A Complete Guide from Foundations to AI-Native Product Leadership

volumes.title: Product Management Foundations
volumes.description: The core discipline of product management — the PM mindset, customer research, prioritization, strategy, metrics, roadmaps, stakeholder management, PRDs, launches, and the operating habits that separate good PMs from great ones.
chapters: Chapter 1: What is Product Management?
Chapter 2: The Product Manager Mindset
Chapter 3: Understanding Customers & Problem Discovery
Chapter 4: Product Discovery
Chapter 5: Product Delivery
Chapter 6: Customer Research & Product Discovery
Chapter 7: Prioritization & Decision Making
Chapter 8: Product Strategy & Vision
Chapter 9: Product Metrics & Measuring Success
Chapter 10: Outcome-Based Roadmaps & Product Planning
Chapter 11: Stakeholder Management & Influence Without Authority
Chapter 12: Writing Effective Product Requirements (PRDs)
Chapter 13: Product Launches & Go-to-Market Execution
Chapter 14: Product Feedback Loops & Continuous Improvement
Chapter 15: Product Anti-Patterns & Common Failure Modes
Chapter 16: The Product Management Operating System

volumes.title: Product Sense
volumes.description: A practical, case-study-driven training ground for product sense and product thinking, built around a structured framework plus more than twenty worked case studies and executive-level mock interviews.

volumes.title: Product Execution
volumes.description: The operational backbone of shipping product — discovery and validation, prioritization, PRDs, agile delivery, analytics, experimentation, launches, and cross-functional leadership.

volumes.title: AI Product Management
volumes.description: A ground-up guide to building AI-native products — LLM fundamentals, prompt engineering, RAG, agents, MCP and tool use, evaluation, safety and governance, cost optimization, and real AI product case studies.
